text: The age-adjusted rate ratio for death was 0.83 (95% CI, 0.75 to 0.93; P<0.001) with dexamethasone among patients receiving invasive mechanical ventilation.
Patients receiving remdesivir had a shorter time to recovery (rate ratio for recovery, 1.29; 95% CI, 1.12 to 1.49; P<0.001).
The rate ratio for death with remdesivir was 0.95 (95% CI, 0.81 to 1.11; P=0.50).
The odds ratio for hospitalization or emergency department visit was 0.30 (95% CI, 0.16 to 0.59; P<0.001) with bamlanivimab plus etesevimab.
The relative risk reduction for hospitalization or death was 70% (hazard ratio, 0.30; 95% CI, 0.17 to 0.53; P<0.001).
The relative risk of COVID-19-related hospitalization or death was 0.11 (95% CI, 0.04 to 0.28) with nirmatrelvir plus ritonavir.
The relative risk of hospitalization or death was 0.70 (95% CI, 0.49 to 0.99) with molnupiravir.
The hazard ratio for hospitalization or death was 0.75 (95% CI, 0.59 to 0.95; P=0.019) with molnupiravir.
The relative risk of hospitalization was 0.68 (95% BCI, 0.52 to 0.88) with fluvoxamine.
The hazard ratio for the primary outcome was 0.69 (95% CI, 0.40 to 1.18; P=0.18) with ravulizumab.
Virologic response occurred in 88% of the dolutegravir group and 81% of the raltegravir group (difference, 7.4 percentage points; 95% CI, 0.7 to 14.2).
The proportion with virologic suppression at week 48 was 91% with dolutegravir-lamivudine versus 93% with dolutegravir-tenofovir-emtricitabine (difference -1.7 percentage points; 95% CI -4.4 to 1.1).
The difference in virologic suppression was -4.1% (95% CI, -10.9% to 2.7%) for dolutegravir plus rilpivirine.
The odds ratio for virologic failure was 0.28 (95% CI, 0.11 to 0.74; P=0.006) with cabotegravir-rilpivirine.
The hazard ratio for HIV infection with cabotegravir versus TDF-FTC was 0.34 (95% CI, 0.18 to 0.62; P<0.001).
